mechanism_narrative: A sustained low federal funds rate reduces discount rates for equity valuations, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in low-rate environments.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and recession risk, causing investors to sell equities and producing negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered instances or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment stays above 5.0 for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and real personal spending confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter through earnings growth expectations.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to produce positive 60-day returns in > 40% of observations or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth decelerates during late-cycle expansions as capacity constraints emerge, triggering short-term equity sell-offs over the next week due to margin compression fears.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive or neutral during late-cycle industrial slowdowns, or the signal reverses direction in early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: High consumer price inflation combined with a low federal funds rate creates a negative real rate environment that forces markets to price imminent aggressive tightening, generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive when CPI exceeds 230 and rates are below 0.5%, or the interaction effect disappears in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts indicate immediate construction sector activity and broader economic momentum, producing positive short-term S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Housing start increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns or show a negative correlation in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth alongside a stable or declining unemployment rate confirms labor market tightness without recessionary breakdown, supporting positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal yields negative returns in > 45% of cases or the interaction term lacks statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where industrial production expands faster than real GDP during post-shock recovery phases signals supply-side resilience, driving positive forward returns as capacity utilization improves.
failure_condition: The supply-demand divergence signal produces negative returns during recovery phases or loses predictive power when applied to normal expansion cycles.
